req.title: Transaction dispute
req.statement: A customer shall dispute a transaction within 60 days of posting; disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a 60-day hard cutoff for transaction disputes. Three partitions exist: (1) age < 60 days — valid dispute window, (2) age > 60 days — rejection zone, (3) age == 60 days — the exact boundary, which must be clarified as inclusive or exclusive. BVA demands testing at day 59 (last valid day), day 60 (boundary), and day 61 (first invalid day). EP covers the interior of each partition. STC captures the state change from 'undisputed' to 'disputed' with provisional credit applied. NEG covers the error pathway producing HTTP 422. A Decision Table test combines multiple conditions (valid auth + expired transaction) to verify the rejection rule fires correctly even when other inputs are valid.

tests.name: test_dispute_created_for_transaction_within_60_days
preconditions: Transaction posted 30 days ago (well within 60-day window)
actions: POST /disputes with valid customer auth token and transaction_id of 30-day-old transaction
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_created_for_transaction_within_60_days(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(age_days=30)
    payload = {"transaction_id": txn["id"], "reason": "unauthorized_charge"}

    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "dispute_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "open"
    assert body["provisional_credit"] is True

tests.name: test_dispute_rejected_for_transaction_older_than_60_days
actions: POST /disputes with valid customer auth token and transaction_id of 90-day-old transaction
expected: Response body contains error code referencing dispute window exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_rejected_for_transaction_older_than_60_days(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(age_days=90)
    payload = {"transaction_id": txn["id"], "reason": "unauthorized_charge"}

    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert "dispute_window" in body["error"].get("code", "").lower() or \
           "60" in body["error"].get("message", "")

tests.name: test_dispute_at_exactly_60_days_boundary
preconditions: Transaction posted exactly 60 days ago (boundary value)
actions: POST /disputes with valid customer auth token and transaction_id of exactly-60-day-old transaction
expected: HTTP 201 Created (inclusive boundary) OR HTTP 422 (exclusive boundary)
Response is deterministic and consistent with server-side boundary definition
If 201: dispute_id present and provisional_credit = true

tests.name: test_dispute_rejected_at_day_61_first_invalid_day
preconditions: Transaction posted exactly 61 days ago (first day beyond window)
actions: POST /disputes with valid customer auth token and transaction_id of 61-day-old transaction
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity regardless of boundary interpretation
Response body contains error referencing dispute window
trace: R-FIN-009: BVA — day 61 is unambiguously outside the 60-day window
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_rejected_at_day_61_first_invalid_day(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(age_days=61)
    payload = {"transaction_id": txn["id"], "reason": "unauthorized_charge"}

    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body

tests.name: test_dispute_state_transitions_undisputed_to_open_with_credit
preconditions: Transaction state is 'undisputed' before the call
expected: provisional_credit is true in dispute response
trace: R-FIN-009: state transition undisputed -> disputed (open) with provisional credit
R-FIN-009: expected outcomes — dispute created; provisional credit applied
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_state_transitions_undisputed_to_open_with_credit(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(age_days=10)

    pre = api_client.get(f"/transactions/{txn['id']}")
    assert pre.status_code == 200
    assert pre.json()["dispute_status"] == "undisputed"

    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json={"transaction_id": txn["id"], "reason": "duplicate"})
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    dispute_id = resp.json()["dispute_id"]

    detail = api_client.get(f"/disputes/{dispute_id}")
    assert detail.status_code == 200
    body = detail.json()
    assert body["status"] == "open"
    assert body["provisional_credit"] is True

tests.name: test_dispute_decision_table_valid_auth_expired_transaction
actions: POST /disputes with valid auth + valid reason + expired transaction_id
expected: HTTP 422 — age rule fires even when all other inputs are valid
Error message references the 60-day dispute window
trace: R-FIN-009: DT — rule: auth=valid AND age>60d AND reason=valid => 422
tests.executable:
def test_dispute_decision_table_valid_auth_expired_transaction(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(age_days=75)
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "reason": "unauthorized_charge"
    }

    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body.get("error", ""))
    assert "60" in error_text or "window" in error_text.lower() or \
           "expired" in error_text.lower()
